title: Factorial Using Recursion
statement:
Write a Python function called `factorial(n)` that returns the factorial of a non-negative integer `n` using recursion.

Read the integer `n` from input and print the result.

Example: factorial(5) = 5 × 4 × 3 × 2 × 1 = 120
starter_code:
def factorial(n):
    # Write your recursive solution here
    pass

n = int(input())
print(factorial(n))
constraints: Do not import any external libraries
